questions.questionText: How many moles of NaOH must be removed from one litre of its aqueous solution to change its pH from 12 to 11?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Pure water has pH 7.0 at 25°C. When the temperature of water is raised to 70°C, then
options: pH will decrease and the water becomes acidic.
pH will increase but the water will still be neutral.
pH will decrease but the water will still be neutral.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following act as acidic buffer?
options: 100 mL of 0.1 M CH3COOH and 100 mL of 0.1 M NaOH
100 mL of 0.1 M NH4OH and 100 mL of 0.05 M HCl
100 mL of 0.1 M CH3COONa and 100 mL of 0.05 M HCl
100 mL of 0.1 M CH3COONa and 100 mL of 0.01 M NaOH
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following solutions will have pH close to 1?
options: 100 mL of M/10 HCl + 100 mL of M/10 NaOH
55 mL of M/10 HCl + 45 mL of M/10 NaOH
10 mL of M/10 HCl + 90 mL of M/10 NaOH
75 mL of M/5 HCl + 25 mL of M/5 NaOH.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If 100 mL of $10^{-2}$ M NaOH solution is mixed with 100 mL of 0.01 M triethyl amine solution ($K_b = 6.4 \times 10^{-5}$), then the change in pH of the resulting solution as compared to the pH of the triethylamine solution will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 37℃, the ionic product of water is $K_w = 2.5 \times 10^{-14}$. Find the pH of a neutral solution at this temperature. (log 2 = 0.3)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Three solutions of strong electrolytes, 25 ml of 0.1 M-HX, 25 ml of 0.1 M-H2Y and 50 ml of 0.1 M-N-Z(OH)2 are mixed, the pOH of the resulting solution is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A certain acid-base indicator is red in acid form and blue in basic form. 80% of the indicator is present in its blue form at pH = 5. The pH at which indicator shows 80% red
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following buffer have maximum buffer capacity?
options: 1 M CH3COOH + 1 M CH3COONa (1 L of each)
2 M CH3COOH + 2 M CH3COONa (1 L of each)
0.1 M CH3COOH + 0.1 M CH3COONa (1 L of each)
0.01 M CH3COOH + 0.01 M CH3COONa (1 L of each)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
1 L of a solution of pH = 2 and 9 L of a solution of pH = 3 is mixed then pH of final solution is

(log 1.9 = 0.28)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Find the pH of a buffer solution having equal volumes of $0.2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ and $0.2 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$. $\left(K_b\right.$ for base $\left.=1.0 \times 10^{-5}\right)$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: 50 mL of 2 M acetic acid mixed with 10 mL of 1 M sodium acetate solution will have an approximate pH of : $\left(K_a=10^{-5}\right)$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Equal volumes of solution with $\mathrm{pH}=6$ and $\mathrm{pH}=8$ are mixed. What will be the pH of resulting mixture?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A certain buffer solution contains equal concentration of $X^{-}$and $H X$. The $K_b$ for $X^{-}$is $1 \times 10^{-10}$. The $p H$ of the buffer is -
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An acid–base indicator, HIn, shows a different colour compared to its conjugate base ( $\mathrm{In}^{-}$). The human eye can clearly notice a colour change only if the ratio $\left[\mathrm{In}^{-}\right] /[\mathrm{HIn}]$ becomes more than 10 or less than 0.1 . What is the least change in the pH of the solution required to see a complete colour change $\left(K_{\mathrm{a}}=1.0 \times 10^{-5}\right) ?$
questions.subject: Chemistry
